header.description: Provide the authentication token obtained from the dashboard

body.raw: // Download the JSON payload from Leegality's dashboard after creating a workflow and paste here

request.description:
A POST call has to made to this API with name, email ID and/or Phone Number of the user(s). A Published Workflow Profile ID needs to be passed in order to successfully create an eSigning request.

If you're using a PDF Type Workflow you can pass a PDF file encoded in Base64 format. If you're using a Template Type Workflow and have fillable fields, you can pass the fields under the fields parameter as available on your dashboard. On successful request creation, you get an ID & eSigning URL in response.

header.description: Provide the authentication token obtained from the dashboard

query.description:
This parameter requires the unique Document ID generated by Leegality for every document in response to a successful create request

*This is a mandatory field

Default Value = false

Pass this parameter as TRUE to receive the PDF file in the API response in a CDN URL. Note: The Content Delivery Network (CDN) URL received in response is valid for a limited time. This URL allows users to download a file from the server within five minutes of its creation. Please note that this URL is intended only for downloading files at the server level, and should not be used for previewing content in the host application. Download Method- curl (file url received from API) -output (path to store the file)

Default Value = false

Pass this parameter as TRUE to receive the Audit trail PDF file in the API response - only applicable to 'Completed' document i.e. Document where all the signatories have signed the document.

Note- Audit trail for a deleted document can also be fetched Note: The Content Delivery Network (CDN) URL received in response is valid for a limited time. This URL allows users to download a file from the server within five minutes of its creation. Please note that this URL is intended only for downloading files at the server level, and should not be used for previewing content in the host application. Download Method- curl (file url received from API) -output (path to store the file)

request.description: You need to call this API with the documentId to get the status of the transaction and/or to fetch the latest version of the document, and the Audit Trail if the document has been completed. Response for the Details API is also the same as the Success Webhook Data apart from the signType parameter. signType parameter will only be provided in the Webhook Data, and not in the Response to the Details API.

item.description:
1. Call this API with the document ID to delete a document.
    
2. Delete an unsigned invitation from a document or delete an invitee group if none  
    if the invitees in the group have signed the document yet.

header.description: Provide the authentication token obtained from the dashboard

item.description:
1. To reactivate expired documents within 30 days of expiry.
    
2. To resend notification with respect to a specific signing URL.
    
3. Before using the API to mark a document as completed, it is important to note that  
    the document must not have any active invitations. To ensure this, please make use  
    of the DELETE API to remove any active invitations.
    
4. eSign sign invitations configured with the sign type "Docsigner". This API is restricted  
    to esigning using Docsigners saved on Leegality's server. Note that this API cannot  
    be used to eSign invitations configured using "Automated Docsigners".A prerequisite  
    to using this API is that you have already created an active eSign invitation using our  
    POST API for creating an eSign request.

item.name: Download document, audit trail, supporting files or attached files

header.description: Provide the authentication token obtained from the dashboard

query.description: The documentID associated with the document you want to fetch. The documentID is shared in response to the API call made to create an eSign request. The documentID can also be retrieved from the Leegality dashboard.
Possible values- DOCUMENT, AUDIT_TRAIL, ATTACHMENT, SUPPORTING_DOCUMENT.

URL of Invitee whose supporting documents is to be fetched.

Pass the invitation URL of the Invitee whose supporting documents you want to receive. The invitation URL is received in response to the API call made to create an eSign request. This parameter is relevant only if the documentDownloadType is SUPPORTING_DOCUMENT.

Note- Only if the documentDownloadType = SUPPORTING_DOCUMENT - if not the value passed will be ignored.

Will be a numeric value indicating which attachment is to be fetched.

Pass the index number of the Supporting Document or Attachment you want to receive. The index starts from 0 with an increment of 1. For example, if you want to receive the 1st supporting document uploaded by an invitee, pass the index value as 0, and if you want to receive the 2nd supporting document uploaded by an invitee, pass the index value as 1, and so on and so forth. This is relevant only if the documentDownloadType is SUPPORTING_DOCUMENT or ATTACHMENT.

Note- Only if Document the documentDownloadType = SUPPORTING_DOCUMENT or ATTACHMENT - if not the value passed will be ignored.

Apart from creating an eSigning request and fetching documents, you can use  
Leegality’s APIs to perform a myriad of tasks.
